content.en:
魔方公式的发展方向

(关于"客观最优"的一些想法)

Jayden McNeill

在过去的一小段时间里针对魔方速拧中的基本情况, 越来越多的小优化浮现在人们眼前, 尤其是OLL / PLL以及拓展到F2L和CMLL之类的情况.

其中许多公式含有骚S, 本位起手的食指F / F', 以避免食指复用为名义的复杂的U层指法选择, 以及其他疯狂的操作.

这些公式通常最多可将时间缩短0.1~0.2s, 同时又会带来不必要的风险, 但是你偶尔会碰到这样一些场景, 要么能节省更多的时间, 要么在保证速度快的同时没那么的大风险.

我还认为, 大多数情况下这些优化都遵循一小部分模式/趋势, 如果你有兴趣找到自己的"客观最优"公式并获得油管上的影响力, 它们很容易识别并加以利用.

下面是我看到的2个主要趋势:

＃1: 这些公式的步数都非常非常少, 以至于它们是STM最优, 或者距离最优STM仅1步;

＃2: 在上述的STM最优/次优公式中, 指法根据起手状态而被精心选择, 一定程度上对本位起手会更加友好……无论是在本位起手还是本位收手, 还是开头的软换手, 或者有一种清晰的方式使得在做其他转动时隐藏必要的换手.

做到＃1并不难, 将情况导入到CE或类似的程序并开启允许中层转动模式, 然后在最先跳出来的几个公式中选一个即可.

＃2就不是那么的容易了, 你需要达到关于指法知识的基础水平的最低限度. 如果你订阅我的邮件通讯足够久, 则你应该熟悉诸如捏, 推, 分离连拨, 以及许多其他内容.

但是有时候, 这些东西还不够, 你真的必须要有一点创造力. 这方面的一个很好的例子是我的f R f' U' r' U' R U M' 指法, 在第一个f之后, 我用右手"手腕"去转接下来的3个转动, R f'和U'.

换句话说, 除了我在2013年学到的一个二阶 CLL之外, 我从未真正在其他情况使用过这个操作.

〜

就目前而言, 许多这些公式似乎相对一文不值, 而且理应如此. 指法越骚, 风险就变得越大.

不过, 这并不是说三阶硬件会变得更糟. 而我们的指法也只会变得更好, 因此我们肯定会看到顶尖魔友最终会从搞"OO"技术的魔友中至少采用一些更有用的技巧.

"OO"是一个文化基因 (译者注: 类似于"梗"), 以及"最优"因其无法被量化而成为令人讨厌的术语, 即便如此, 我们推进可能的极限的唯一方法就是探索这些东西.

〜

将情况改变到足够明显的程度 & 相信这个步骤, 一切都会变得更好

-J

词汇

OO (Objective Optimal) 客观最优的

funky 时髦的, 骚的

clout 影响力

勘误: disjointed doubleflicks不是左右手连拨, 而是分离连拨, 相当于eido手法的中间插了一步. 例如Ra perm公式(R U' R' U') (R U R D) (R' U' R D') R' U2 R'的U' R' U'部分. 以下来源speedsolving论坛:

A disjointed half turn (different types of this can be called disjointed double flick, disjointed eido etc.) describes the fingertrick of performing a half turn that in the middle is interrupted by a different turn, effectively splitting the half turn into two quarter turns. This is done to make the two quarter turns more fluid and prevent even small regrips.

An example of this would be a disjointed double flick as it appears in the well-known T perm, R U R' U' R' F R2 U' R' U' R U R' F'. The two marked U' moves form this disjointed double flick. When the algorithm is executed, instead of performing a U' flick, the R' and then another U' flick, the solver performs a double flick with the R' in between, which saves the small regrip. This means that the first U' is done with the left index finger, then the R' is performed and finally, the last U' is executed with the left middle finger, which means that the two U' moves are executed the same way as in a normal double flick, but with a longer pause between them.

Where Algs Are Heading

(some thoughts on the whole "objectively optimal" thang)

Jayden McNeill – https://www.jaydenmcneillcubing.com/

Over the past little while, we've seen an influx of more and more minor optimizations for basic cases you get in a speedsolve, most notably with OLL/PLL as well as things like F2L and CMLL to an extent as well

Many of these algs include funky S slices, home grip style index F/F' turns, sophisticated U layer fingertrick choices in the name of avoiding reloading/overworking your index fingers, and other crazy brazy stuff

Often times these algs lead to a .1-.2 time improvement at best while introducing unnecessary risk, but you get the occasional gem that either saves more time, or genuinely isn't that risky to execute while still being faster

I also think that for the most part, these optimizations all follow a small set of patterns/trends that are easy to identify and even capitalize on if you're interested in finding your own "objectively optimal" algs and getting some YouTube clout

These are the 2 primary trends I'm seeing

The movecount for these algs is always very very low, to the point where they're either optimal in STM, or within 1 move of STM optimal

The fingertricks are a result of carefully choosing the starting angle of said STM optimal(ish) algorithm in a way that facilitates some level of home grip friendliness... whether it starts & finishes in home grip, or it has a soft regrip at the start, or there's a clear way to hide necessary regrips while doing other turns during the alg

Getting trend #1 right is really just a matter of plugging cases into CE or something similar, turning slice moves on, and picking one of the first algs that pop out

Trend #2 isn't so easy & is where you need a base level of fingertricks knowledge at the absolute minimum. If you've been following my newsletter for a long enough period of time, you should be familiar with ideas like pinch turns, push turns, disjointed doubleflicks, and a bunch of other stuff

Sometimes however, that stuff isn't enough and you genuinely have to get a little creative. A good example of this is my f R f' U' r' U' R U M' fingertrick where after the first f, I use my right hand to "wrist turn" the following 3 turns, R f' & U'

In other words, using a fingertrick that I've never really used for any other case ever, other than a random 2x2 CLL I learned in like 2013 or something

~

For the time being, many of these algs are going to seem relatively worthless, and rightfully so. The fancier a fingertrick gets, the riskier it becomes

Still, it's not like 3x3 hardware is getting any worse, and our fingertrick abilities should only be getting better, so we're definitely going to see the top cubers adopt at least some of the more useful tricks from the "OO" crowd eventually

Even though "OO" is a total meme term and optimal is impossible to quantify making it an obnoxious terminology, the only way we push the limits of what's possible is by exploring this stuff

~

Move the needle & trust the process

-J
